title: Theoretical vs Experimental Probability
subject: Mathematics
learningOutcomes: Differentiate between theoretical and empirical (experimental) probability.
Simulate coin tosses, dice rolls, and card draws.
Observe the Law of Large Numbers convergence across 1,000 trials.

objectives.text: Run single coin flips and observe individual outcomes.
Execute a batch of 100 trials to observe convergence toward 50%.

theory: Theoretical probability $P(E) = m/n$ is calculated based on sample space. Experimental probability $P_{exp}(E)$ is calculated from actual observed trials. By the Law of Large Numbers, $P_{exp}(E) \to P(E)$ as trials $N \to \infty$.
hints: Click "+100" or "+1000" to run batch trials instantly.
Check the Convergence Analysis card to compare rates.

tasks.instruction: Run at least 100 trials to test convergence.

observationPrompts: Why does 10 trials often deviate from 50%, whereas 1000 trials stays near 50%?

realWorldExamples.title: Weather Forecasting
realWorldExamples.description: Meteorologists calculate experimental probability using historical atmospheric simulation data.

commonMistakes: Expecting small sample sizes (e.g. 10 flips) to yield exact theoretical ratios.
